world_history.pre_reckoning_note: History before Year 0 is measured in rough Ages. Poorly understood, mythologized, politically contested. Different powers claim descent from different pre-Reckoning civilizations to legitimize themselves.

year_zero_event.name: The Silence After Varath
year_zero_event.description: The First Reckoning calendar was established by survivor-scholars of the Varathi Collapse. A civilization called Varath — total, vast, now obliterated — collapsed in a manner still disputed. The scholars of Year 0 agreed on nothing except that the world before should not be counted the same way as the world after.
year_zero_event.what_was_varath: Unknown. Disputed. Mythologized. Politically exploited. The ruins are real. The cause of the collapse is not agreed upon.
year_zero_event.legacy: Varathi ruins scatter the continent of Ossrath and appear elsewhere. They are quarried for materials, built upon, mythologized. Scholars from other continents find the locals' casual relationship with them deeply distressing.

five_great_turnings.name: The Bleeding Century
five_great_turnings.summary: The world emerging from the Varathi Collapse fragments into survivor-states. The First Reckoning scholars attempted a League of successor states across two continents. It lasted 340 years before fragmenting over resource access and succession disputes.
five_great_turnings.conflict: The Bleeding Century — roughly 180 years of wars fought with salvaged Varathi weapons nobody fully understood how to maintain.

plague.name: The Weeping Rot
plague.type: Hemorrhagic, likely waterborne via contaminated war-camp water

legacy: Varathi ruins become permanent landscape fixtures
The League's failure poisons the idea of multinational governance for two thousand years

five_great_turnings.name: The Iron Theocracy and its Unmaking
five_great_turnings.summary: The Ordinate of the Eternal Furrow — a theocratic empire — unified most of one continent through genuine agricultural innovation and brutal slave labor. Its expansion triggered the Coalescence Wars.

ruling_power.name: The Ordinate of the Eternal Furrow
crimes: Enslaved roughly one third of the population

five_great_turnings.conflict: The Coalescence Wars — a loose alliance of free city-states, nomadic confederations, and breakaway slave armies. Lasted nearly four centuries.
five_great_turnings.collapse_cause: Self-inflicted famine through over-farming exhausted land. The collapse of its agricultural infrastructure starved two continents simultaneously.
legacy: Religious institutions viewed with permanent structural suspicion
The Ordinate's irrigation works, half-ruined, still function in places — claimed by whoever controls the land above them

five_great_turnings.name: The Sundering Fleets / The Drowned Centuries
five_great_turnings.summary: A maritime era — multiple thalassocracies dominating trade across four continents. Ended by simultaneous volcanic catastrophe and cascading naval wars.
collapse_cause: The Ashveil — a chain of volcanic eruptions along a major tectonic seam, disrupting growing seasons for ~40 years
Naval wars triggered by trade route collapse as the thalassocracies fought over diminishing resources

plague.name: The Grey Shivers
plague.type: Respiratory illness, possibly worsened by Ashveil particulate matter in the atmosphere

legacy: Several major ports abandoned — now ruins below sea level or reclaimed by wetland
Sailors still use corrupted fragments of thalassocratic charts

five_great_turnings.name: The Century of Knives / The Long Betrayal
five_great_turnings.summary: No single great war. A slow-motion catastrophe of internal collapse across multiple independent powers simultaneously. Sustained over roughly nine centuries.
causes: Grain hoarding by nobility during famine years
A religious schism splitting the world's dominant faith into three mutually hostile branches

plague.name: The Slow Hunger
plague.type: Likely a wasting disease — possibly multiple overlapping diseases mythologized into one. Became so endemic it was background reality for three generations.

legacy: Succession law elaborately codified — and frequently violated
Political fault lines still technically active — ended only ~5,000 years ago

five_great_turnings.name: The Ashen March / The Generation That Did Not Sing
five_great_turnings.summary: The most recent Turning. A single power — the Brennac Hegemony — conquered roughly 60% of the known world through organizational and logistical superiority. Collapsed through administrative overextension and succession war.

ruling_power.name: The Brennac Hegemony
ruling_power.rise: Two centuries of conquest through superior logistics and administration — better at feeding armies, moving supplies, and governing conquered territory
ruling_power.fall: Administrative collapse — too large to govern, provincial governors acting independently, three-way succession war shattered the administrative spine in a single generation

five_great_turnings.the_ashen_march: Decades of roving ex-Hegemony armies with no employer and no home, stripping the landscape as they moved.

plague.name: The Coughing Black
plague.type: Almost certainly pneumonic plague. Moved with the roving armies.

legacy: The current political map is almost entirely defined by what filled the Hegemony's vacuum
Many of the 42 major powers are successor states, breakaway provinces, or entities formed in opposition to Hegemony remnants
The Brennac administrative system — roads, census methods, legal codes — was so good that many powers still use corrupted versions
People alive today have great-great-grandparents who were Hegemony soldiers

current_era.description: Four centuries out from the last collapse. Things are recovering unevenly. The 42 powers are in various states of consolidation, expansion, and internal stress. Nobody wants another Turning. Everyone is doing things that might cause one.
current_era.no_mans_lands: The spaces between powers are filling back in with desperate homesteaders, bandit confederations, and minor lords trying to carve out something new. These are the most dangerous and most free places in the world.
current_era.macro_tension: Something from the Varathi Collapse is surfacing. It is touching multiple regions simultaneously through different vectors. Most characters will never find out what it is. Some will find a piece of it. Nobody will find all of it.
